rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Answer In Intervention
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 28, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Specially Appearing Industrial Indemnity Company and United States Fire Insurance Company's Motion for Leave to File An Answer in Intervention is granted pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure section 387. No opposition. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT JAMES HANSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Answer In Intervention And Mpa
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 28, 2019 in Department 503, Line 5. A 170.6 challenge to Judge Cynthia Ming-Mei Lee was exercised on 7/6/15. Matter to be heard by Judge Andrew Cheng on Tuesday at 10:15 am in Dept 606.

rulings.Case Title: MARY BETH PERNIZ VS. DURO DYNE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Insufficient Service Of Process
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 28, 2019 in Department 503, Line 6. Intervenor American States Insurance Company (A Safeco Company)'s Motion to Dismiss for Insufficient Service of Process is denied. Intervenor lacks standing to bring this motion as it failed to file an answer-in-intervention within 30 days of the April 30, 2019 hearing on its Motion to Set Aside Default and Seek Leave to File an Answer-In-Intervention, as stated in the Court's order. The Court did not consider Intervenor's moving papers or reply papers for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court. (Local Rule 2.7(B).) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
